title: We Give Thee But Thine Own
author: William Walsham How
story: Bishop How, known as 'the poor man's bishop' for his work in London's East End, wrote this stewardship hymn reminding Christians that all we have belongs to God.

lines: We give Thee but Thine own, Whate'er the gift may be
All that we have is Thine alone, A trust, O Lord, from Thee
And we believe Thy Word, Though dim our faith may be
Whate'er for Thine we do, O Lord, We do it unto Thee
May we Thy bounties thus As stewards true receive, And gladly, as Thou blessest us, To Thee our firstfruits give
And we believe Thy Word, Though dim our faith may be
Whate'er for Thine we do, O Lord, We do it unto Thee
O hearts are bruised and dead, And homes are bare and cold, And lambs for whom the Shepherd bled Are straying from the fold
And we believe Thy Word, Though dim our faith may be
Whate'er for Thine we do, O Lord, We do it unto Thee
To comfort and to bless, To find a balm for woe, To tend the lone and fatherless Is angels' work below
And we believe Thy Word, Though dim our faith may be
Whate'er for Thine we do, O Lord, We do it unto Thee
The captive to release, To God the lost to bring, To teach the way of life and peace
And we believe Thy Word, Though dim our faith may be
Whate'er for Thine we do, O Lord, We do it unto Thee
